reviews.review_msg: Amazing prof, very accessible, everything you need to know is on the slides. After taking the class, I felt that I actually learnt something. Do practice quizzes, he provides a lot of practice material (do that). MCQs are tricky but similar to quizzes, SA question: pathways, predicting results on experiments (understand this).

Awesome Young Guy, teaches well with practice questions every lecture! Prepares you well for exams! Course is heavy but dheva made it so much fun! He was accessible outside of class via email and office hours too! I dropped during unscheduled time and he did not mind! Problem sets are the best way to study!

Dr. J is a great prof who really cares about his students success. It was a pleasure to take a course with him. The course itself is def heavy so I recommend you prepare well and study, use your TA for extra resources and ask questions!

The course itself is pretty difficult due to an intensive amount of information, but what made it less horrible for students was his grading scale where an 80% is already an A-, unlike other inconsiderate professors who normally set 80% or even 81% for a B+. Additionally Dr J is considerate and easy-going, and his accent is just addictive.
Overall, he was a really good prof and his lectures were super engaging. The exams were a little difficult and time crunch, but he would scale the grades accordingly.

Dr. Jaumouille is a great lecturer. His accent is not that hard to decipher, and he compensates for it by talking a little bit slower. He explains things very clearly, and is passionate about the lectures he gives and the topics he teaches. The grading scheme for the class is 3 exams and weekly assignments, which is pretty standard for MBB at SFU.

Professor was professional in the field but not good at explaining and teaching. Sometimes he sounded not confident enough to explain the slides. Slides did not have enough explanations but some of those were on the exams. 

Final exam was changed from non-cumulative to cumulative so it was a bit heavy. MC questions were long (Rmb to spend time)

Dr. Jaumouille delivers his lectures well and they are all recorded so you can review them anytime. Exams are pretty decent with some difficult questions here and there but he scales the grade. Make sure to review learning objectives in his slides, attend tutorials and you should be fine. Overall, I would take another class with him again.

Great professor! Her lectures were engaging while discussing fairly dull content. She quickly answers every question asked in lecture in excellent depth and shows real compassion for her students. Exams are tough but extremely reasonable considering the course content and the marking is fair - would 100% take a course with Dr. Sneddon again.

Dr. Sneddon is probably one of best professors I've ever had. This semester has been pretty depressing and whenever I go to Dr. Sneddon's class I know that it'll be great because she has such a kind and amazing personality. The class was similar to your usual MBB class but the prof made it amazing and I'm so happy I took this class with her.
Best Prof I had this semester, would take every class with Dr. Sneddon if I had the chance

This course was pretty interesting once we got into cancer and stem cells near the end, and Dr Choy explained things pretty well. This course is based off only exams that require a pretty detailed understanding of concepts to do well. Averages were kind of low, but grades were scaled a few percent in the end!

Dr. Choy is great at explaining concepts. He also provides recorded lectures which are very helpful.His exams are fair as long as you go over all the stuff that he provides. His exams are a solid medium.I think listening to his lectures and understanding the slides is all you need to do well in this course.

He used a kinda roundabout way of explaining things. He seems nice on the surface level but he is a tough grader. I got the basics but as someone else said on here he marks on the specifics even though said specifics aren't on the slide or are but unnoticeable. Couldn't even find where or when he had office hours as the slides mentioned nothing.

I was not sure what to expect of Dr. Choy based on his low ratemyprof score. However I was pleasantly surprised and I believe he has significantly improved his teaching. His lectures are super clear, and his presentation made a ton of sense. You are graded purely off exams but they focus more on big picture and compared to other classes are doable.

Seems very unprepared when lecturing. Doesn't seem to know what is on his slides. Luckily lectures were recorded. Would have been a pain to commute to campus for an extremely dry lecture. It is easy to do well in this course if you're good at memorizing. Exam questions were very straight forward.

Dr. Choy usually just reads off the slides and doesn't provide too much more information beyond that. He can be a bit confusing at times when he teaches during lectures. There are very few sources that he provides in his classes that allow for extra practice, so you'll be stuck having to look for other sources. Not bad but provides the bare minimum

Talks way too much but isn't very clear about the information. Much confusion as he reads through his slides. Could be easily taught but speaks too much, making everything confusing. This could've been so interesting with the information but he makes it very boring. Makes it all about memorization and wastes too much time. Not conveniently taught.
very unorganized.
No Comments
No Comments

She is the worst teacher I have had so far.  She is nice, but... her notes are disorganized... adds slides just before the lecture...(and doesn't add them to webct)... and doesn't give you enough time to copy them... but... she is the worst teacher simply because her exams are poorly written.
Hard
Does not know how to teach. She is everywhere with her lecture. Notes are really unorganized, but that goes for her as well. Her tests were way out there.

Notes are veri messy and herself too. My guess is that half the time, she doesn't know what she is saying herself. She laugh at her joke while everyone else dead silence. Exams are hard but do-able. Notes aren't really detailed, and comes A LOT of abbreviations. SO prepare to dig your textbook up if you plan to take this course.

Her exams are very unfair. There are some "choice" questions that are worth 6 marks??? She is a knowledgeable professor, but she just can't explain the material with clarity. I suggest an alternative prof, if possible, but I think she teaches half of this course every time.
No Comments
for her unorganized notes...but she's a very nice person though...she just can't teach...
Attend tutorials and lecture review days!!! Hard work pays off, exams are extremely fair but remember everyone does well, keeping up with material really helps especially in tutorial

I don't think I ever saw Dr. Northwood in a bad mood once. She's super friendly and easy to engage with. Her lectures are very digestible. Her grading was a little on the difficult side. Some tests she tried to make "easier" but wound up making them harder; at least for me and I may or may not be stupid lol. She was super clear about final material
great prof in MBB courses, i have taken 321,322 with Northwood, grades obtained are good and fair. Easy workload, attendance matters to good grade.

Northwood is a pretty knowledgeable professor and creates a fun/relaxed environment. She relates material to real world stuff if you don't like the usual bland mbb material. Super clear on what's on the exam and so tests are relatively easy with the averages being around 76%.
awesome prof....very helpful, very to the point
Hilarious, hysterical, sometimes indecisive. Good prof, though.

CRAP professor! She teaches you nothing. All you have to do is memorize what she tells you will be on the exam. You get nothing from taking a course with her. Her advising abilites are questionable and she treats students with disrespect, especially if you are not in MBB!!
Nice lady, a bit scatter brained.  Tells what to know/what not to know for exams.  Exams made up of many short answer questions, so really memorize everything she says to.

she tells u what u need to know for the exam..and that's what u really need to concentrate on in terms of exam prep...everything she covered has been straight memorization as it is mostly the case in other mbb courses
Her exams are very easy: short answer (one word to one sentence) questions.  And she tells you EXACTLY what will be on the exam - no suprises.

For MBB 201 - Mark is the goat. Although the content of the course can be dry sometimes, it is taught in a clear, concise way that makes it clear what he is going to test you on. Exams are very fair and he even gave bonus marks for our 2nd midterm. Highly recommend

Dr. Brockman seems to really care about his students. However, his lectures were confusing and his tests were much more detailed than he suggested they would be in class. Reading the textbook is a must, because he includes very specific details on the tests.

Mark is brilliant, friendly and cares that his students do well. The lecture material is dry and going to class is optional. Exams require a lot of memorizing. Big concepts for written but the multiple choice does test on some very small details (protein names) Final was simple - just study what is on his review sheet, no more than that.

Dr. Brockman is an amazing professor, he is very clear in every topic for each of the lectures and will help if you ask! His exams are fair but study the book and his notes vigorously if you want to do well in his exams! Everyone clapped at the end of his last lecture, that's how awesome he is!
Exams are very fair with only a few trick questions.  Class averages were very high although attendance was quite low.  Study notes and should be okay.

I found the material really interesting, but like most people I rarely attended lecture because he read straight off the notes. The material isn't really hard, but the class average was quite high on all exams, so you really have to know your stuff to do really well.
Dr. Brockman is a great professor who is very interested in what he teaches. He allowed us a cheat sheet on the final, which made the exam very easy. Recommended.

First time teach 322 this sem. He's very nice and has good notes. As for exams, its mostly fair with some trick questions. Had 3 midterms. Final exam was cumulative and much harder but was allowed one cheat sheet. Half the ppl dont show up to lecs cause its boring. Just study the notes well and you will do decent. Overall a good prof.

Dr. Hawkins was the most considerate and caring prof that I have encountered during the pandemic. She goes through material at a relatively slow pace in lecture, taking time to explain concepts which I really appreciate. Exams are very very fair.

Nancy is great at teaching the difficult and boring stuff you want to skip by. She goes over it so it make sense and is more than willing to help if the topic does not make sense.  Going to lecture is KEY! The notes she provides via Powerpoint are great, but you need to go to fully grasp what she wants you to away from that slide.
Love, love, love her!!!
Great professor! Her lectures are pretty interesting and it's great to see a professor who is really passionate about her course. Exams are fairly basic so studying the notes should be enough.

She is NOT an okay proff! It really depends on the person. If your the type of person that can memorize everything in the notes, then take this class. BUT if you cannot memorize every single thing on the slides, then DO NOT TAKE HER CLASS! Im not good at memorization, so am not doing too well. :S
 Also, the only people that do good in her class are the ones that LOVE MBB. If you dont, then DONT TAKE IT!!!

She is one of the best profs in MBB department. She is very organized and she makes things very clear. Her review classes are super helpful in preparing midterms and final. Textbooks weren't really needed; lecture notes have all the information. Her exam touches on almost EVERYTHING so studying everyday makes a real difference!

She really knows her stuff.very interested in the stuff she teaches. midterms are a little on the harder side.Final wasn't hard but it was cumilitive and she screwed everyone up by giving a lottttttttt of questions on the first and second section material of the course.she is very organized. oh and she does not scale even one bit!
